name: Braciole Alla Messinesi
method: In 3 small bowls, combine breadcrumbs, garlic, pecorino, parsley, salt, and pepper in one, flour in another, and a beaten egg in the third bowl. On a cutting board, pound steak very thin, you must roll it easily for the braciola. Cut 2 inches wide and 4 inches long. Coat both sides with flour, egg, then breadcrumbs, and lay on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Line up your stripes and on each one place a slice of provolone, cut it to fit. Roll and secure with a toothpick. In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil and fry each piece until golden brown on both sides. Add more oil if needed. Place on a platter. Serves 2-3. Olé
